# Contributing

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-05 -->

## Prerequisites

| Tool | Version | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Ruby | 3.2, 3.3, or 3.4 | Tested in CI against all three (see `.circleci/config.yml`) |
| Bundler | 2.3.26+ | Install with `gem install bundler:2.3.26` |
| RubyGems | latest | Ships with Ruby |

No Docker required. No external services required — the gem has zero runtime dependencies and all tests are unit tests.

## Getting Started

```bash
# Clone
git clone git@github.com:contentful/rich-text-renderer.rb.git
cd rich-text-renderer.rb

# Install dependencies
bundle install    # source: Gemfile → gemspec

# Run tests
bundle exec rake spec    # source: Rakefile → RSpec::Core::RakeTask.new

# Run linter
bundle exec rake rubocop    # source: Rakefile → RuboCop::RakeTask.new
```

## Development Workflow

Use Guard for a file-watching dev loop (auto-runs specs and rubocop on save):

```bash
bundle exec guard    # source: Guardfile
```

Or run the combined check in one shot:

```bash
bundle exec rake rspec_rubocop    # source: Rakefile → task rspec_rubocop: %w(spec rubocop)
```

## Commands

### Testing

```bash
bundle exec rake spec             # source: Rakefile → RSpec::Core::RakeTask.new
bundle exec rake test             # source: Rakefile → task test: :spec (alias)
bundle exec rspec spec/path/to/specific_spec.rb    # run a single spec file
```

RSpec output format is `--color --format documentation` (source: `.rspec`).

### Linting

```bash
bundle exec rake rubocop          # source: Rakefile → RuboCop::RakeTask.new
bundle exec rubocop -A            # auto-fix safe offenses
```

### Combined (CI equivalent)

```bash
bundle exec rake rspec_rubocop    # source: Rakefile → runs spec + rubocop
```

### Documentation

```bash
bundle exec yard doc              # source: rich_text_renderer.gemspec → guard-yard dev dependency
```

YARD options are in `.yardopts`.

### Release

```bash
bundle exec rake release          # source: Rakefile + rubygems-tasks gem
```

Requires RubyGems credentials from 1Password (`CF-Ruby-Chapter` vault) and OTP. See the [Ruby SDK Runbook](https://contentful.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ECO/pages/5499847157) for the full release procedure.

## Testing

- **Framework:** RSpec ~> 3 (source: `rich_text_renderer.gemspec`)
- **Location:** `spec/lib/rich_text_renderer/` — mirrors `lib/rich_text_renderer/` structure
- **Coverage:** SimpleCov (source: `spec/spec_helper.rb` — disabled on JRuby)
- **Run all:** `bundle exec rake spec`
- **Run single:** `bundle exec rspec spec/lib/rich_text_renderer/renderer_spec.rb`
- **Watch mode:** `bundle exec guard` — uses `guard-rspec`

### Test Helpers

`spec/spec_helper.rb` defines `MockAsset` and `MockFile` helper classes for testing asset renderers without a real Contentful SDK dependency, plus example Markdown renderer classes for integration tests.

## Code Style & Conventions

- **Linter:** RuboCop ~> 1.62.1 (source: `rich_text_renderer.gemspec`)
- **Config:** `.rubocop.yml` + `.rubocop_todo.yml` (auto-generated suppressions)
- **Excluded from RuboCop:** `spec/**/*`, `Guardfile`, `Gemfile`, `Rakefile`, `*.gemspec`
- **Signal exception style:** `only_fail` — use `fail` not `raise` (source: `.rubocop.yml`)
- **Max class length:** 250 lines (source: `.rubocop.yml`)
- **Encoding cop:** disabled (source: `.rubocop.yml`)

## Commit Convention

This repo uses a loose convention (no enforced commitlint). Observed patterns from git history:

```
type: description
type(scope): description
```

Common types: `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `docs`, `refactor`

Examples from history:
```
feat: add superscript and subscript text renderers
fix: handle nil nodes in BaseNodeRenderer#find_renderer
chore: update codeowners and catalog-info.yaml to DX
docs: update changelog
```

## Branch Strategy

- `master` — main branch; CI runs on push; releases tag from here
- Feature branches — use descriptive names (e.g., `rubydog/rich-text-tables`, `chore/dx-687`)
- PRs target `master`

## Release Process

Releases are manual and owned by Bhushan Lodha (contractor):

1. Bump `lib/rich_text_renderer/version.rb`
2. Update `CHANGELOG.md` (uses `v` prefix, e.g., `v0.3.3`)
3. Run `bundle exec rake release`
4. Requires RubyGems credentials + OTP from 1Password
5. Git tag is created automatically; GitHub Releases are NOT used in this repo

See [Ruby SDK Runbook](https://contentful.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ECO/pages/5499847157).

## Pull Requests

- Target `master`
- CI runs `bundle exec rake rspec_rubocop` across Ruby 3.2, 3.3, and 3.4 (source: `.circleci/config.yml`)
- CodeQL scans GitHub Actions workflow changes (source: `.github/workflows/`)
- CODEOWNERS: `@contentful/team-developer-experience` (source: `.github/CODEOWNERS`)
- **Known gap:** CI does not automatically run for PRs from forked repos (DX-808). Maintainer must trigger manually.

## CI/CD

| Job | Trigger | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `test_and_lint` | Push/PR to `master` | Runs `bundle exec rake rspec_rubocop` across Ruby 3.2, 3.3, 3.4 (source: `.circleci/config.yml`) |
| `CodeQL Scan` | Push/PR to `master` touching `.github/workflows/` | Scans GitHub Actions workflow files for vulnerabilities (source: `.github/workflows/`) |

CI alerts route to `#sdk-bots` Slack channel (source: `catalog-info.yaml`).

## File-Level Guidance

| Path | Notes |
|---|---|
| `lib/rich_text_renderer/version.rb` | Version constant — bump as part of release process only |
| `.rubocop_todo.yml` | Auto-generated by `rubocop --auto-gen-config` — do not hand-edit |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Update with every release; uses `v` prefix on version headers |
| `rich_text_renderer.gemspec` | Gem metadata and dependencies — changes here affect what gets published |
| `lib/rich_text_renderer/renderer.rb` | `DEFAULT_MAPPINGS` — adding a new Contentful node type means adding an entry here and a corresponding renderer class |
